description: Talbot arrives unexpectedly at his family home. He shows off his new gravitonium-based abilities to his son George, aiming to impress him and win back his family. However, when Carla mentions S.H.I.E.L.D., Talbot's temper flares and he uses his power to threaten her, foreshadowing his loss of control and alienation from those he loves.

transcript.line: Hello, George. Daddy's home. What's the matter? Cat got your tongues?
I'm just surprised. With everything going on in the world, I... I didn't know... You look...
Yeah. Fit as a fiddle. Never better. I'm a new man. Clear-headed and healthy.
Why are you dressed so funny?

Well, this is my new uniform, son. Your dad's a superhero now, just like those Avengers. And guess what? I even have my own superpowers. That's just a taste. I can move buildings and cars. And you want to know something else? I can even fly. And I don't need a hammer or an iron suit to do it.

Absolutely. Things are gonna be different from now on. Your daddy's all better. And I'm not gonna let anything bad happen to you or anyone else. I'm gonna chase those invaders out of town. Now, you ready to go for that flight?
George, honey, why don't you go up to your room and play so your father and I can talk?
Not now, Carla. We're having fun.
Glenn, please. We need to talk. I heard about the accident.
What the hell are you talking about?
S.H.I.E.L.D. Sorry. Look what you made me do.
I'm really sorry, Glenn. They're just worried about you. That's all. And so am I. George, please, honey, go upstairs.
I'm sorry, George. I can fix it, though. Better yet, I can take you to a real spaceship. What do you think about that, partner?
